{{Infobox location
| name = Subspace
| introduced = The Captain
}}

''See also: [[Subspace Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Subspace is an extra-dimensional realm that serves as the primary method for accurate long-distance travel. Accessing this dimension requires a specialized [[Subspace Drive]], though the process carries significant risks; mechanical failures can lead to drive ruptures, and traveling without a vessel can reduce a person to something less than a dream. The physical experience of entering Subspace involves intense pressure, often described as being squeezed through a straw. While the surface layer can appear as pure darkness, deeper regions are characterized by vibrant, swirling colors, such as rainbow hues or fractured amethyst patterns. The environment within Subspace is highly volatile and dangerous. Diving into the dimension pulls surrounding matter along with the vessel, which can create violent storms of uncontained gas that threaten to tear ships apart. Large vessels are particularly vulnerable, as they cannot safely dive while within a planet's atmosphere and face greater strain due to the amount of matter they drag with them. Furthermore, Subspace is inhabited by creatures that can sense a ship's drive and may attempt to consume it for nourishment. Beyond physical hazards, Subspace presents psychological and communicative challenges. Long-range communication becomes unreliable or garbled while submerged in this dimension, though certain entities like [[Titans]] possess advanced receivers. The visual patterns of the realm can also cause "[[Subspace madness]]," a state where individuals become so captivated by the sights that they lose their way. While most travelers use it for rapid transit, the dimension's depth and complexity remain a significant barrier to exploration.}}

== The Captain ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|Subspace is an isolated pocket dimension and a multicolored twist of reality where targets of the [[Absolute Burial]] spell are sent. This extra-dimensional medium serves as a vital means for long-distance transportation, though it is a region where prediction fails to work precisely and astral megafauna stalk the area. While ships can dive into this state to evade enemies or avoid annihilation, they must use sealing magic to remain protected and can be prevented from sinking into the dimension by mundane materials. The bridge of [[The Last Horizon]] is located within this space, which allows for the manifestation of turrets and the materialization of [[Omega]]. Ultimately, Subspace acts as a strategic gateway for attacking the [[Grand Hive]], provided one enters from outside the [[Iron King]]'s awareness, and it was powerful enough to draw half of the [[Grand Hive]] into its depths.}}

<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">27</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Subspace]] is an isolated pocket where targets of the [[Absolute Burial]] spell are sent.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c3">''The Captain'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Karoshan ships]] can dive into [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c6">''The Captain'', chapter 6</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The mountain was sealed in [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c8">''The Captain'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Subspace]] is a dimension or state that [[Omega]] can slide in and out of.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c10">''The Captain'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Subspace]] is a pocket dimension.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c14">''The Captain'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The ship can dive into [[Subspace]] to evade enemies.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c16">''The Captain'', chapter 16</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|Accurate long-distance transportation is considered impossible without going through [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c16" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]] can use [[Subspace]] to travel.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c23">''The Captain'', chapter 23</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|Turrets on [[Horizon]] manifest out of [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c24">''The Captain'', chapter 24</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|Ships can emerge from [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c24" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Subspace]] is a dimension or state that ships can emerge from.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c27">''The Captain'', chapter 27</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Subspace]] is a place or state that reality can twist into.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c27" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Horizon]] uses sealing magic to protect the ship in [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c28">''The Captain'', chapter 28</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]] can materialize from [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c29">''The Captain'', chapter 29</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Subspace]] is a place one can dive into to avoid annihilation.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c30">''The Captain'', chapter 30</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Subspace]] is a multicolored twist that ships can escape through.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c31">''The Captain'', chapter 31</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Subspace]] is a region where astral megafauna stalk.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c32">''The Captain'', chapter 32</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The bridge of [[The Last Horizon]] is located in [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c35">''The Captain'', chapter 35</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|Mundane materials can prevent a ship from sinking into [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c35" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Subspace]] is a medium through which ships travel.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c38">''The Captain'', chapter 38</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Subspace]] is an extra-dimensional space where prediction does not work precisely.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c38" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|To attack the [[Grand Hive]] successfully, one must enter [[Subspace]] from outside the [[Iron King]]'s range of awareness.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c39">''The Captain'', chapter 39</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The crew plans to dive back to [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c41">''The Captain'', chapter 41</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Last Horizon]] emerged from [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c42">''The Captain'', chapter 42</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Subspace]] is a dimension or area that characters can enter.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c42" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Subspace]] is a place or state that creatures can flee into.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c44">''The Captain'', chapter 44</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|Half of the [[Grand Hive]] was drawn into [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c45">''The Captain'', chapter 45</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== The Engineer ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Subspace is a multicolored river of chaos used by fleets as a method of travel, though it can distort sound and cause madness in organic [[life]]. While the [[Lichborn heavy shuttle]] once traveled through this medium, [[Starhammer]] was forced to exit the dimension early to avoid carrying unprotected civilians into its unpredictable depths. The realm is dangerous enough that it can cause mortals to warp and twist in strange dimensions, and the [[Shadow Ark]] eventually imploded into its chaotic expanse. Beyond its physical dangers, calling [[Divine Titans]] within Subspace is a tricky endeavor, and interference from the dimension has even rendered footage of [[Varic]] inconclusive. Even [[Omega]] utilizes this state for utility, employing Subspace storage to hold his gun.}}

<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">13</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Subspace]] is a state or dimension that [[Starhammer]] must avoid entering while carrying unprotected civilians.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c1">''The Engineer'', chapter 1</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Subspace]] is a location or state where calling [[Divine Titans]] is tricky.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c9">''The Engineer'', chapter 9</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Subspace]] is a medium through which the [[Lichborn heavy shuttle]] traveled.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c12">''The Engineer'', chapter 12</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Subspace]] is a chaotic realm that can cause madness in organic [[life]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c16">''The Engineer'', chapter 16</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Mell]] can return the ship to [[Subspace]] in a couple of hours.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c19">''The Engineer'', chapter 19</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Subspace]] is a dimension or method of travel used by fleets.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c34">''The Engineer'', chapter 34</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] exited [[Subspace]] early.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c37">''The Engineer'', chapter 37</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[The Shadow]] [[Ark]] imploded into [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c38">''The Engineer'', chapter 38</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Subspace]] is a multicolored river of chaos.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c45">''The Engineer'', chapter 45</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Subspace]] can distort sound.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c45" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] uses [[Subspace]] storage to hold his gun.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c50">''The Engineer'', chapter 50</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Subspace]] interference caused inconclusive footage of [[Varic]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c51">''The Engineer'', chapter 51</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Subspace]] can cause mortals to warp and twist in strange dimensions.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c52">''The Engineer'', chapter 52</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== The Knight ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Subspace serves as a dimension and method of travel that can be accessed through ruptures or blooming warps in outer space. While it functions as a storage location for [[Omega]]'s guns and entire fleets, the realm contains a cold, hungry [[void]] that can be blocked or used to send entities off-course. During their journey, the crew makes it their objective to reach this dimension, eventually diving into it via a command to the ship. Within this realm, magical soldier ants emerge on shimmering wings, and it is a place from which the [[Green Knight]]'s [[Divine Titan]] can fall. The [[D’Niss]] utilize Subspace for movement, and it is a medium through which certain spells can reach, as well as a location where [[Javik]] can summon a two-handed cannon.}}

<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">17</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Subspace]] is a place where [[Omega]] stores his guns.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c3">''The Knight'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Subspace]] is a medium or dimension that certain spells can reach through.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c5">''The Knight'', chapter 5</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Subspace]] is a location where entities can be attacked and sent off-course.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c9">''The Knight'', chapter 9</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Fleets can be kept in [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c14">''The Knight'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The characters will be in [[Subspace]] for a while.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c15">''The Knight'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Subspace]] is a dimension or route used for travel that can be blocked.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c16">''The Knight'', chapter 16</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Subspace]] is a method of travel and contains a [[void]] that is cold and hungry.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c16" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Javik]] can summon a two-handed cannon from [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c20">''The Knight'', chapter 20</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[Green Knight]]'s [[Divine Titan]] falls from [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c20" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The narrator commands the ship to dive into [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c28">''The Knight'', chapter 28</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Subspace]] warps can bloom in outer space.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c35">''The Knight'', chapter 35</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The crew's objective is to reach [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c36">''The Knight'', chapter 36</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Subspace]] can be accessed via a rupture.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c36" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Subspace]] is a realm that can be entered via a [[Subspace warp]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c38">''The Knight'', chapter 38</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Soldier]] ants emerge from [[Subspace]] on shimmering magical wings.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c39">''The Knight'', chapter 39</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Raion]] was believed to be in [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c42">''The Knight'', chapter 42</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[D’Niss]] used a method of [[Subspace]] travel to move.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c44">''The Knight'', chapter 44</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== The Pilot ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Subspace is an inherently unstable dimension or state of reality that serves as a unique fold for isolation. While its surface consists of pure darkness, rainbow colors shine through cracks in the shadows and patterns of yellow lights can be viewed via helmet feeds. This dark pocket allows [[Aila]] to pull items from its depths, while [[Omega]] can drop into the dimension to evade telekinesis. Although the [[Aether Core]] preserves enough magic for use within this realm, Subspace contains sanity-shattering depths where nameless abominations originate. The dimension is used by ships to wait for drive stabilization, though certain enemies can pass through it as individuals and the [[Captain]] possesses the ability to lock people inside.}}

<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">26</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Aila]] can pull items from [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c1">''The Pilot'', chapter 1</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] can drop into [[Subspace]] to avoid telekinesis.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c8">''The Pilot'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Aila]]'s clones used [[Subspace]] to dive into.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c14">''The Pilot'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Subspace]] is a state or dimension that [[The Last Horizon]] can enter.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c16">''The Pilot'', chapter 16</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Vallenar crew]] surfaced from [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c18">''The Pilot'', chapter 18</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The surface of [[Subspace]] is made of pure darkness.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c18" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Aether Core]] preserves enough [[Aether]] to allow the use of magic in [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c19">''The Pilot'', chapter 19</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Subspace]] existence is inherently unstable.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c20">''The Pilot'', chapter 20</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The characters have insights on the creatures of [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c21">''The Pilot'', chapter 21</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Captain]] can lock individuals into [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c21" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Subspace]] is a state or dimension that ships can be out of while waiting for their [[Drive]] to stabilize.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c24">''The Pilot'', chapter 24</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|A [[Subspace warp]] blooms just outside [[Fathom]]’s atmosphere.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c24" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Nameless abominations originate from the depths of [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c25">''The Pilot'', chapter 25</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Sola]]'s missiles emerge from [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c25" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Subspace]] is a dark pocket that can be accessed.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c29">''The Pilot'', chapter 29</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Subspace]] contains rainbow colors beneath its shadows.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c29" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Subspace]] is a dimension that shines through cracks in shadow.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c29" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Reception in [[Subspace]] is not good.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c30">''The Pilot'', chapter 30</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Subspace]] is a place with sanity-shattering depths.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c36">''The Pilot'', chapter 36</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Subspace]] is a dimension or state that [[Benri]] felt drawn into during his descent.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c38">''The Pilot'', chapter 38</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Subspace]] is a destination or state [[Benri]] cannot reach if the door is warped.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c39">''The Pilot'', chapter 39</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Subspace]] is a unique fold of reality used for isolation.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c43">''The Pilot'', chapter 43</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Subspace]] is a medium that certain enemies can pass through as individuals.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c45">''The Pilot'', chapter 45</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Subspace]] can be viewed through a feed routed into a helmet.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c46">''The Pilot'', chapter 46</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Subspace]] contains patterns of yellow lights.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c46" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] might bolt into [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c47">''The Pilot'', chapter 47</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|“Shouldn’t take me more than a couple hours to get us back in Subspace.”<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c19" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|"This is it," Shadow Ark said to him, the sound weirdly distorted in the swirls of Subspace.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c45" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{The Last Horizon nav}}

[[Category:Locations]]
[[Category:The Captain]]
[[Category:The Engineer]]
[[Category:The Knight]]
[[Category:The Pilot]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon locations]]
[[Category:Otherworldly locations]]
